lenses.guidance: v 4 — 'do not muzzle an ox while it is treading out the grain' — is quoted at 1 Cor 9:9 and 1 Tim 5:18 as warrant for paying gospel workers. The canonical thread runs from threshing-floor regulation to Pauline ministry ethics.

vv 17-19's Amalek-remember command bookends the chapter against vv 1-3's flogging-limit. The structural framing pairs measured discipline within Israel against unmeasured destruction of the merciless enemy at the wilderness rear.

vv 5-10's levirate marriage — kinsman raises seed for the dead brother — is the pattern Ruth 4 draws on (Boaz the kinsman-redeemer) and Mt 1:5 names in Christ's genealogy. The shadow prefigures redemption through kin.
